fact: February 1 is exactly 32 days into the year, which means you've already lived through more than 8% of the entire year!
In the Northern Hemisphere, groundhogs are typically getting ready to peek out of their burrows - their body temperature has been dropping to just above freezing all winter!
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day around February 1st in most places, so you're gaining almost a quarter-hour of daylight each week!
February 1 falls in the middle of Aquarius season - the water bearer constellation that looks like a person pouring out a giant jug!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, February 1 is peak summer and some eucalyptus trees are literally exploding their seed pods in the heat!
Arctic foxes are at their fluffiest right now - their winter coats are so thick they can sleep comfortably on snow at -40 degrees!
In cold places, tree sap is frozen solid like popsicles inside the bark, but the trees are secretly getting ready to wake up in just a few weeks!
February 1 is exactly halfway between the winter solstice and spring equinox - ancient people called this cross-quarter day 'Imbolc'!
Polar bears in the Arctic are typically giving birth to tiny cubs right now - the babies are only the size of hamsters and completely blind!
The jet stream winds high above Earth are usually at their strongest around February 1, creating wild weather patterns down below!
In places like Canada, the snow is often so cold and dry it squeaks like styrofoam when you walk on it - scientists call this 'snow squeak'!
